publications

Publications by categories in reversed chronological order.

2020

  1. Lightweight approach to automated fault diagnosis in WSNs Swain, Rakesh Ranjan, Dash, Tirtharaj, and Khilar, Pabitra Mohan IET Networks 2020 [HTML]

2019

  1. A complete diagnosis of faulty sensor modules in a wireless sensor network Swain, Rakesh Ranjan, Dash, Tirtharaj, and Khilar, Pabitra Mohan Ad Hoc Networks 2019 [HTML]
  2. A comprehensive study on evolutionary algorithm-based multilayer perceptron for real-world data classification under uncertainty Dash, Tirtharaj, and Behera, Himansu Sekhar Expert Systems 2019 [HTML]
  3. Neural network based automated detection of link failures in wireless sensor networks and extension to a study on the detection of disjoint nodes Swain, Rakesh Ranjan, Khilar, Pabitra Mohan, and Dash, Tirtharaj J. Ambient Intell. Humaniz. Comput. 2019 [HTML]
  4. Discrete Stochastic Search and Its Application to Feature-Selection for Deep Relational Machines Dash, Tirtharaj, Srinivasan, Ashwin, Joshi, Ramprasad S., and Baskar, A. In Artificial Neural Networks and Machine Learning - ICANN 2019: Deep Learning - 28th International Conference on Artificial Neural Networks, Munich, Germany, September 17-19, 2019, Proceedings, Part II 2019 [HTML]
  5. Investigation of RBF Kernelized ANFIS for Fault Diagnosis in Wireless Sensor Networks Swain, Rakesh Ranjan, Dash, Tirtharaj, and Khilar, Pabitra Mohan In Computational Intelligence: Theories, Applications and Future Directions - Volume II 2019 [HTML]

2018

  1. Adversarial neural networks for playing hide-and-search board game Scotland Yard Dash, Tirtharaj, Dambekodi, Sahith N, Reddy, Preetham N, and Abraham, Ajith Neural Computing and Applications 2018 [HTML] [Code]
  2. Fault diagnosis and its prediction in wireless sensor networks using regressional learning to achieve fault tolerance Swain, Rakesh Ranjan, Khilar, Pabitra Mohan, and Dash, Tirtharaj International Journal of Communication Systems 2018 [HTML]
  3. Multifault diagnosis in WSN using a hybrid metaheuristic trained neural network Swain, Rakesh Ranjan, Khilar, Pabitra Mohan, and Dash, Tirtharaj Digital Communications and Networks 2018 [HTML]
  4. Large-Scale Assessment of Deep Relational Machines Dash, Tirtharaj, Srinivasan, Ashwin, Vig, Lovekesh, Orhobor, Oghenejokpeme I., and King, Ross D. In Inductive Logic Programming - 28th International Conference, ILP 2018, Ferrara, Italy, September 2-4, 2018, Proceedings 2018 [HTML]

2017

  1. Sequence-based discrimination of protein-RNA interacting residues using a probabilistic approach Pai, Priyadarshini P., Dash, Tirtharaj, and Mondal, Sukanta Journal of Theoretical Biology 2017 [HTML] [Code]
  2. An effective graph-theoretic approach towards simultaneous detection of fault(s) and cut(s) in wireless sensor networks Swain, Rakesh Ranjan, Dash, Tirtharaj, and Khilar, Pabitra Mohan International Journal of Communication Systems 2017 [HTML]
  3. A study on intrusion detection using neural networks trained with evolutionary algorithms Dash, Tirtharaj Soft Comput. 2017 [HTML]
  4. Image Captioning-Based Image Search Engine: An Alternative to Retrieval by Metadata Iyer, Sethurathienam, Chaturvedi, Shubham, and Dash, Tirtharaj In Soft Computing for Problem Solving - SocProS 2017, Volume 2, Bhubaneswar, India, December 23-24, 2017 2017
  5. Genetic Algorithm-Based Oversampling Technique to Learn from Imbalanced Data Saladi, Puneeth Srinivas Mohan, and Dash, Tirtharaj In Soft Computing for Problem Solving - SocProS 2017, Volume 1, Bhubaneswar, India, December 23-24, 2017 2017 [Code]
  6. GASOM: Genetic Algorithm Assisted Architecture Learning in Self Organizing Maps Saboo, Ashutosh, Sharma, Anant, and Dash, Tirtharaj In Neural Information Processing - 24th International Conference, ICONIP 2017, Guangzhou, China, November 14-18, 2017, Proceedings, Part I 2017 [HTML] [Code]
  7. Towards Continuous Monitoring of Environment under Uncertainty: A Fuzzy Granular Decision Tree Approach Reddy, Preetham N., Dambekodi, Sahith N., and Dash, Tirtharaj In Joint Proceedings of the 3rd Modelling Symposium (ModSym), Developmental Aspects of Intelligent Adaptive Systems (DIAS), and Educational Data Mining Practices in Indian Academia (EDUDM) co-located with 10th Innovations in Software Engineering (ISEC 2017), Jaipur, India, February 5, 2017 2017 [HTML] [Code]

2016

  1. Evolutionary Neural Networks versus Adaptive Resonance Theory Net for Breast Cancer Diagnosis Nayak, Tanistha, Dash, Tirtharaj, Rao, D. Chandrasekhar, and Sahu, Prabhat K. In Proceedings of the International Conference on Informatics and Analytics, ICIA 2016, Pondicherry, India, August 25-26, 2016 2016

2015

  1. Automatic navigation of wall following mobile robot using Adaptive Resonance Theory of Type-1 Dash, Tirtharaj Biologically Inspired Cognitive Architectures 2015 [HTML]
  2. Gradient gravitational search: An efficient metaheuristic algorithm for global optimization Dash, Tirtharaj, and Sahu, Prabhat K. J. Comput. Chem. 2015 [HTML]
  3. Controlling Wall Following Robot Navigation Based on Gravitational Search and Feed Forward Neural Network Dash, Tirtharaj, Nayak, Tanistha, and Swain, Rakesh Ranjan In Proceedings of the 2nd International Conference on Perception and Machine Intelligence, Kolkata, West Bengal, India, February 26-27, 2015 2015 [HTML]
  4. Hybrid Gravitational Search and Particle Swarm Based Fuzzy MLP for Medical Data Classification Dash, Tirtharaj, Nayak, Sanjib Kumar, and Behera, H. S. In Computational Intelligence in Data Mining - Volume 1 2015 [HTML]

2014

  1. GA Based Polynomial Neural Network for Data Classification Nayak, Janmenjoy, Sahoo, N., Swain, J. R., Dash, Tirtharaj, and Behera, Himansu Sekhar In 2014 International Conference on Information Technology, ICIT 2014, Bhubaneswar, India, December 22-24, 2014 2014
  2. An Experimental Study of a Novel Move-to-Front-or-Middle (MFM) List Update Algorithm Mohanty, Rakesh, Dash, Tirtharaj, Khan, Biswadeep, and Dash, Shiba Prasad In Applied Algorithms - First International Conference, ICAA 2014, Kolkata, India, January 13-15, 2014. Proceedings 2014 [HTML]
